Answer:
Portable NaI(Tl) Scintillator-Based Environmental Gamma-Ray Spectroscopy System
1. Overview
This PCB serves as the main acquisition unit for a portable NaI(Tl) scintillator-based environmental gamma-ray spectroscopy system. It integrates the ZYNQ7020, ADC, DDR memory, and front-end amplification circuits, supporting spectrum acquisition, signal processing, and data transmission.
2. Main Control Chip  
The core processor is a Xilinx ZYNQ7020 SoC, which includes a dual-core ARM for running Vitis-based control logic, while the programmable logic (PL) side handles high-speed data processing and shaping algorithms.
3. Signal Acquisition  
Analog signals from the NaI(Tl) detector and photomultiplier tube (PMT) are passed through a charge-sensitive preamplifier, pole-zero cancellation (PZC) network, and peak detection circuitry, then digitized by a high-speed onboard ADC.
4. Data Processing  
The FPGA implements a complete digital pulse processing chain, including pulse detection, leading-edge identification, baseline estimation, trapezoidal shaping, pile-up rejection, peak extraction, and spectral shaping.
5. Data Communication  
Processed spectral data is transferred via AXI Stream interface to the ARM core, then sent to the host PC or mobile terminal through DMA and interrupt-driven UART transmission.
6. Power Supply  
Multiple AMS1117 regulators provide 3.3V, 1.8V, and 1.0V power to support ZYNQ core, DDR, ADC, and analog circuits. Analog and digital domains are separated using AGND and GND to minimize noise interference.
7. Memory Resources  
Onboard DDR memory is used to buffer pulse and waveform data, ensuring continuous acquisition and high throughput.
8. Debugging and Control  
The board includes multiple buttons such as RST and SW configuration switches. It also features a USB-to-serial chip for firmware flashing and a JTAG header for logic-level debugging.
9. PCB Layout  
The board has a compact footprint, approximately the size of a bank card. Analog and digital sections are logically partitioned. Copper pours can be toggled for visual inspection, and differential pair routing ensures signal integrity in critical paths.

Answer:
YQ-PCS Low-Power Analyzer  
1. Overview
This is a portable micro power meter designed specifically for current measurement in low-power electronic devices. It features high sampling rate, high upload speed, auto-ranging protection, and dynamic waveform visualization, making it suitable for behavior analysis and test verification in R&D scenarios.
2. Core Performance Specifications
2.1 Measurement Range  
Supports current measurement from 0 to 10A (recommended ≤10A) and voltage measurement from 0 to 30V.
2.2 Sampling and Upload Rate  
Sampling and reporting rates range from 10KS/s to 100KS/s, suitable for observing fast pulses or wake-up events.
2.3 Communication Interface  
Uses Type-C serial communication. The old version supports 2.5M baud rate, while the new version uses 4M baud rate with a CH343P chip, enabling high-speed data transmission and real-time waveform uploading.
2.4 Interface Isolation  
All interfaces are electrically isolated to ensure measurement accuracy and system safety.
3. Current Auto-Ranging System
3.1 Ranging Levels  
There are 8 current ranges in total, covering 4μA to 40A (the 7th level is theoretical maximum; recommended ≤10A for practical use).
3.2 Resolution  
Minimum resolution reaches 0.001μA, maximum range resolution is 10mA. Range switching delay is controlled within 3μs for all levels.
3.3 Accuracy  
After calibration, static accuracy across all levels is better than 0.5%, depending on the precision of the calibration instrument.
3.4 Temperature Drift  
Under room temperature, drift is well-controlled. In high-temperature environments, some fluctuations may occur. Recommended as a reference only.
4. Voltage Auto-Ranging System
4.1 Ranging Levels  
Includes 4 voltage ranges: 0–4.096V, 0–8.192V, 0–29.4912V, and 0–37.6833V.
4.2 Resolution  
Minimum resolution is 1mV, maximum is 9.2mV. Range switching takes one sampling cycle.
4.3 Accuracy  
Accuracy is also better than 0.5%, sufficient for most voltage dynamic observation tasks.
4.4 Default Range Setting  
Factory default auto-ranging starts from level 1. Level 0 must be manually activated.
5. Circuit and Hardware Design
5.1 Main Control Scheme  
The latest version adopts the STC32G series MCU paired with a CH343P USB-serial chip to improve communication stability and eliminate previous data misalignment issues.
5.2 Clock and Capacitor  
Crystal oscillator and load capacitors are reselected to ensure stability at high baud rates.
5.3 Power Input Method  
Supports dual-side input terminals (left/right), allowing flexible adaptation to different test setups and physical reversal of input direction.
5.4 Fault Protection Mechanism  
Includes features such as fault shutdown, error range detection, reverse input protection, enhancing system reliability.
6. Accuracy Testing and Performance
6.1 Static Error Testing  
Using a 1Ω 0.1% sampling resistor as reference, the measured static average current closely matches oscilloscope results, with good error control.
6.2 Dynamic Waveform Performance  
At 50K sampling rate, current waveform details are highly restored, allowing accurate analysis of pulse currents and wake-up events.
6.3 Signal Fluctuation  
Measured signal fluctuates around 7% of full scale, which is within acceptable limits. Variance is well-controlled.
6.4 Usage Recommendation  
High sampling rates are recommended for precise waveform capture; low rates are suitable for average current monitoring.
7. Host Software Features
7.1 Waveform Display  
Real-time voltage and current waveform display with support for X-axis time compression and Y-axis scaling.
7.2 Data Processing  
Provides average calculation, range switching status, and dynamic trend tracking.
7.3 Communication Configuration  
Allows manual selection of baud rate and COM port. Automatically identifies device model and firmware version.
7.4 Version Compatibility  
New PC-side software is only compatible with the new firmware. Ensure hardware and software versions match during updates.

Answer:
ESP32 Multi-NAS IoT Information Monitoring Device
1. Overview
This is a multifunctional IoT monitoring terminal PCB based on the ESP32 module, specifically designed for DIY NAS and soft router environments. It supports centralized monitoring of network status, system resources, and environmental parameters, addressing the lack of front-end visualization in traditional NAS setups.
2. Main Control Unit  
The board integrates an ESP32-WROOM module as the core controller, featuring WiFi connectivity and Bluetooth support, suitable for data acquisition and transmission across multiple systems.
3. Functional Features  
3.1 Real-time monitoring of multiple NAS devices, including network connectivity, CPU usage, memory load, disk activity, fan operation, and voltage levels  
3.2 Supports external temperature and humidity sensors (e.g., AHT20 or SHT series) for monitoring the environmental conditions around the device  
3.3 Equipped with a 0.96-inch OLED display to show multiple status pages with clear and intuitive UI  
3.4 Includes real-time clock and weather display features for enhanced usability  
3.5 Simplified operation with no complex configuration, suitable for long-term stable operation
4. Circuit Resources  
4.1 Display interface connected via FPC to an ST7796-driven color LCD panel  
4.2 Supports GT911 touch controller for basic interactive input  
4.3 Onboard CH343K USB-to-serial chip for programming and debugging  
4.4 Uses AMS1117-3.3 voltage regulator for stable power supply to main controller and peripherals  
4.5 Reserved I2C and GPIO headers for easy expansion with additional sensors or modules  
4.6 Includes BOOT and RESET buttons and exposes multiple user-defined input pins  
4.7 Dedicated PCB layout for WiFi antenna routing to ensure optimal wireless signal quality
5. Application Scenarios  
5.1 Monitor DHCP status and enclosure temperature of IstoreOS-based main router  
5.2 Perform connectivity testing and system monitoring for OpenWRT-based soft routers  
5.3 Monitor virtual machines and Docker containers running on Unraid systems, including disk usage, temperature, and fan status  
5.4 Display storage and system health status for DSM-based NAS virtual machines
6. Design Significance  
This PCB integrates network communication, display, human-machine interaction, and environmental sensing in one compact form, making it ideal for front-end monitoring and environmental control of NAS hosts, soft routers, and similar devices. It effectively enhances system management efficiency and operational stability.

Answer:
USB3.0 Card Reader Based on GL3224 Solution Supporting Camera SD Cards
1. Overview
This PCB is based on the GL3224 solution, supporting USB3.0 transfer and compatible with UHS-I SD card specifications. The design includes a reserved external Flash solder pad for future firmware upgrades. The device has been tested with Kingston 256G Blue card and Samsung ProPlus 128G Red card (2019 version), with read speeds reaching 90-100M/s. The PCB is 1mm thick, and the final product thickness is only 4mm, making it extremely thin.
2. Technical Specifications
- Chip Model: GL3224
- Interface Type: USB3.0 Micro-B interface
- Supported Specifications: Supports UHS-I SD cards
- External Expansion: Reserved external Flash solder pad for future firmware upgrades
- Tested Cards: Kingston 256G Blue card, Samsung ProPlus 128G Red card (2019 version)
3. Functional Module Description
3.1 USB Interface Module
- Provides a Micro-B3.0 interface, supporting USB3.0 data transfer. Users can connect using a regular external hard drive cable or purchase specific cables (e.g., Samsung S5 or Note3 original cables).
3.2 SD Card Slot Module
- Supports full write protection recognition. If the purchased card slot does not support write protection recognition, users need to manually fly the write protection pin to GND for proper data writing.
4. Jumper Function
This design does not use jumper functions; all connections are made through external interfaces and solder pads.
5. Usage Instructions
- When connecting the device, ensure that the cable used supports USB3.0, such as a Micro-B interface data cable.
- After inserting the SD card, the device can perform data reading and writing. If the SD card does not support write protection recognition, jumper settings may need to be adjusted.
6. PCB Layout and Routing
- The layout is compact, with a PCB thickness of 1mm and a final product thickness of 4mm, ensuring the device is extremely thin and portable.
- Power, data transfer, and SD card slot routing use high-quality traces to ensure stability and high transfer speed.

Answer:
Hanwen HelloWord-Keyboard
1. Overview
This is a multifunctional, modular mechanical keyboard PCB designed to meet personal usage needs. It supports hot-swappable functional modules, features independent communication capabilities and extensibility, and is suitable for embedded interaction and multitasking input scenarios.
2. Board Structure Design  
2.1 Overall Composition  
The system consists of three main parts: the docking base, the keyboard input module, and the interchangeable interaction module. These parts connect via contact pads for power and communication, and can operate independently or in combination.  
2.2 Keyboard Input Module  
Utilizes a matrix scanning design based on shift registers to optimize key wiring and resource usage. The current layout is a 75-key configuration derived from the Xikii S98 design and can be modified as needed for other layouts.  
2.3 Interchangeable Interaction Module  
The default module is Dynamic, which includes an E-ink display and an FOC force-feedback rotary encoder. It supports status display, menu control, and parameter tuning. The module can be replaced with user-defined components.  
2.4 Docking Base  
The base provides mechanical alignment, electrical interconnection, and serial communication bridging, ensuring reliable hot-swapping of modules.
3. Circuit and Hardware Architecture  
3.1 PCB Structure  
The overall circuitry consists of 10 sub-PCBs, covering keyboard control, power management, module interfaces, encoder driving, etc. Designed using Lichuang EDA, with a board thickness of 1.2mm.  
3.2 Main Control Chip Configuration  
An ARM Cortex-M-based MCU is used as the main controller to handle key scanning, module data exchange, and peripheral logic control.  
3.3 Communication and Electrical Interfaces  
Modules communicate with the main controller via serial protocols, supporting bidirectional data exchange and command invocation. The expansion pads are compatible with various module extensions.  
3.4 Clock and Timing  
The layout ensures proper placement of the main crystal oscillator and coupling capacitors, supporting stable communication at high baud rates. Firmware allows flexible serial baud rate configuration.
4. Firmware and Functional Support  
4.1 Keyboard Main Firmware  
The firmware implements key scanning, keycode mapping, function layer logic, and supports macro programming, custom key combos, and lighting control.  
4.2 Dynamic Module Firmware  
The module runs on an independent MCU, supporting interface rendering, rotary input processing, and synchronization with the main board.  
4.3 Secondary Development Interface  
Both keyboard and module firmware expose SDK interfaces, enabling users to develop and load custom apps with extended interaction logic.  
4.4 Standalone Module Capability  
Modules can function as standalone interactive devices, communicating with the main keyboard or external hosts via UART.
5. Functional Expansion Features  
5.1 Hot-Swappable Modules  
Module interfaces support hot-swapping without power-off, suitable for frequent scene switching.  
5.2 Multi-Module Chaining  
The PCB supports multiple modules connected in series via UART, forming interactive matrices or multi-channel control systems.  
5.3 State Synchronization Mechanism  
The main controller can poll module states via protocol, and modules can actively report interaction events.  
5.4 Typical Interaction Applications  
Common use cases include system status display, power and temperature monitoring, volume control, scene switching, and code snippet input.
6. Usage and Development Suggestions  
6.1 Applicable Scenarios  
Suitable for DIY enthusiasts, HCI developers, desktop productivity tool designers, or as a prototyping platform.  
6.2 PCB Customization Flexibility  
Users can modify key layouts, power interfaces, and component placement while retaining the original scanning circuit.  
6.3 Firmware Compatibility Notes  
Custom layouts require updates to scanning logic and mapping tables. Module functions must align with the main controller's protocol definitions. 
6.4 Recommended Development Tools  
Keil MDK and STM32CubeMX are recommended for main firmware development. Modules can be developed using PlatformIO or Keil, with logic analyzers aiding in UART debugging.

Answer:
Homekit Multi-functional Environmental Sensor Board  
1. Overview
This PCB is a smart sensor board designed for home automation applications. It integrates light, temperature, humidity, and human presence detection functions, and is based on the ESP8266 (ESP-12F) main control module.
2. Functional Module Composition  
2.1 Main Control System  
The board uses an ESP8266 module (ESP-12F package) as the core controller. It includes a serial download interface for firmware flashing and debugging. Onboard RST and GPIO buttons allow reset and basic user interaction.
2.2 Radar Sensor Interface  
A 2×4 pin header (labeled TX1, RX1) is reserved for connecting a human presence radar sensor. The interface supports different radar module types.
2.3 Temperature, Humidity, and Light Sensors  
Designated areas on the PCB are used for small package sensors that measure temperature, humidity, and ambient light. Sensor data is collected via the I²C bus.
2.4 Power Supply System  
The board supports 5V DC input. Multiple onboard LDO regulators convert power to 3.3V for the main controller and sensors. The 5V and 3.3V power rails are clearly separated in the PCB layout.
2.5 Communication and Debug Interfaces  
The board includes a standard serial download interface (TXD/RXD/GPIO0) and a Micro-USB power input. It supports UART-based communication between modules and allows firmware updates.
2.6 PCB Structure  
The board is designed as a double-layer PCB with dimensions of approximately 38mm × 38mm. The compact layout is suitable for embedded applications. Four mounting holes are reserved for mechanical installation.
3. Additional Features  
Supports cooperative operation of multiple sensors  
Clearly labeled signal pins and debug headers for easy expansion and testing  
Double-sided SMT layout for high integration

Answer:
XMOS USB Digital Audio Interface  
1. Overview
This PCB is a USB-to-I2S audio interface board based on the XMOS xCore-200 series XU208-128-QF48 chip. It is designed to convert USB audio signals into I2S format, suitable for high-fidelity digital amplifiers. The board features a compact design and by default supports 32bit/192kHz PCM audio transmission. Higher sampling rates and DSD mode are theoretically supported via firmware updates.
2. Functional Modules  
2.1 Main Controller  
The core is the XU208-128-QF48-C10 chip (QFN48 package), which integrates a high-speed USB 2.0 PHY to simplify circuit design.
2.2 Power System  
The board requires 3.3V I/O voltage and 1.0V core voltage:  
3.3V is supplied via LDO regulator, while 1.0V is provided by a DC-DC converter to reduce power consumption and heat.
2.3 Clock System  
Uses the Accusilicon AS318B crystal oscillator (2520 package), which is compact, cost-effective, and stable, making it suitable for high-precision audio applications.
2.4 Flash Storage  
Firmware is stored in SPI NOR Flash (e.g., ZD25WQ80BTIGT 8Mbit). The actual firmware size is about 27KB, so even a 512Kbit Flash is sufficient. Note that XU208 supports both SPI and QSPI modes, and correct wiring and code configuration are required according to the datasheet.
2.5 Reset Circuit  
Power-on reset is handled by the BU4209G TR chip, used here as a replacement for the original NCP303LSN09T1G design.
2.6 Output Interface  
A 2×10Pin header with 2.0mm pitch is used as the I2S audio output interface, allowing connection of multiple signal lines. The connection method can be optimized as needed.
3. Board Structure  
3.1 PCB Layers and Size  
Designed as a 4-layer PCB with dimensions of 40mm × 22mm. The compact structure is suitable for embedded integration.
3.2 Daughterboard Form  
The board is designed as a daughterboard, making it easy to pair with mainboards and adaptable for modular audio system development.
4. Usage Instructions  
4.1 Default Features  
The default firmware supports 32bit/192kHz PCM audio output, suitable for most high-fidelity applications.
4.2 Extended Features  
Theoretically supports PCM 768kHz and DSD512, but firmware modification and compilation are required to enable these modes.
4.3 Audio Input Capability  
Audio input functionality can be added. This is a rare feature among most commercial digital audio interfaces.
4.4 System Integration  
This board can be used in combination with a TAS5825M digital amplifier developed by the author, forming a complete digital audio chain.
5. Development Notes  
5.1 Resource Access  
All development tools, software libraries, code examples, and tutorials are available on the XMOS official website after registering an account.
5.2 Flash Compatibility  
When using SPI or QSPI Flash, ensure that the protocol and main controller settings match to prevent connection and firmware issues.
5.3 Development Advantages  
Compared to the Italian Amanero design and commercial CMedia/CT/SA9227 solutions, the XU208 offers greater flexibility, open documentation, and a smaller BOM footprint.

Answer:
V1.2 ESP32 Solar Water Heater Interface for Home Assistant
1. Overview
This PCB is an interface board based on the ESP32C3 module, designed to integrate traditional solar water heater systems into smart home platforms such as Home Assistant. With ESPHome firmware configuration, it supports real-time monitoring of water temperature and water level, and enables remote control and automation of heating and water refilling functions. The board is compatible with conventional heater controllers, offering high cost-performance and flexible installation.
2. Functional Modules  
2.1 Main Control System  
The control core adopts the ESP32C3 module from Lichuang (LuatOS version), featuring built-in WiFi for wireless smart home applications. As some commercially available documentation may have incorrect pinouts, it is recommended to refer to the official documentation for accurate pin configuration and firmware flashing.
2.2 Sensor Interface  
The board provides interfaces for temperature and water level sensors, compatible with the original four-wire sensor of the water heater controller. It enables synchronized data display between the controller and Home Assistant. Temperature measurement accuracy is within ±2℃, and further compensation can be applied via software for enhanced precision.
2.3 Control Outputs  
Supports control over the original system’s automatic heating and water refilling functions without interference. Current-limiting resistors are added to control signal paths to match the controller's signal voltage (around 4V). It is advised to measure the actual signal voltage and adjust resistor values accordingly before use.
2.4 Power Supply System  
Powered via a 12V DC input, sharing the same power line with the water heater controller. For safety during debugging, a 12V adapter can temporarily replace the 220V AC power, minimizing the risk of operating under high voltage.
2.5 Terminal Connections  
There are three sets of terminals for sensor input and control output. The sensor interfaces use 2×2P round pin headers and sockets for convenient disassembly. Later versions have optimized the connector types to improve wiring reliability and structural integrity.
3. Board Structure  
3.1 Physical Design  
The board features a slim and long layout suitable for most heater controller enclosures. To save space and maintain tidy routing, the ESP32C3 module is soldered directly to the main board without using pin headers.
4. Usage Instructions  
4.1 Installation  
Fix the soldered board inside the heater controller and complete the wiring according to the schematic and firmware notes. It is important to verify the electrical characteristics of sensors and control lines to ensure proper pin mapping.
4.2 Firmware and Configuration  
Use ESPHome to compile and flash the firmware. Refer to the official ESP32C3 pinout instead of third-party diagrams to avoid functional errors caused by incorrect configurations.
4.3 Safety Precautions  
As the heater controller contains 220V AC circuitry, all operations must be performed with power disconnected. Proper electrical knowledge is required for wiring and testing. Do not operate or measure under live conditions.
5. Additional Notes  
5.1 Debugging Advice  
To minimize risks during testing, a 12V DC supply can simulate the operating environment of the heater, enabling safe wiring and function verification. If the sensors used have resistance values that differ from common market types, software-level calibration may be applied to ensure accurate readings.

Answer:
Five-Port Gigabit Ethernet Switch Board
1. Overview
This PCB is a five-port gigabit Ethernet switch board based on the RTL8367RB chip. It is designed for high-speed internal network data transmission, making it suitable for applications such as soft routers, NAS, and custom local area networks. Its primary function is to facilitate high-throughput, low-latency data switching without relying on external internet access.
2. Functional Modules  
2.1 Switch Chip  
The core of the board is the RTL8367RB chip from Realtek, which supports five gigabit Ethernet ports, VLAN segmentation, and MII extension. The board exposes relevant MII interface pins to support connection with external processors and further expandability in MAC/PHY modes.
2.2 Power System  
The board requires both a 3.3V I/O voltage and a 1.0V core voltage, each supplied through separate LDO regulators. A 5V 2A power adapter is recommended to ensure stable operation when all five ports are active. Avoid using a 5V 1A adapter as it may cause damage.
2.3 Network Interfaces  
The board integrates five RJ45 connectors with built-in transformers, labeled RJ1 through RJ5. These support full-duplex transmission, auto-negotiation, and MDI/MDIX auto-switching. The compact layout allows deployment in embedded systems.
2.4 Thermal Management  
Under full load (e.g., simultaneous large file transfer across all five ports), power consumption can reach 4.8–4.9W, with significant heat generation. Inadequate cooling may lead to speed drops from gigabit to 100 Mbps. Heat sinks (aluminum or copper) are recommended to maintain performance stability.
2.5 MII Expansion  
The board exposes GMAC1 and GMAC2 MII MAC/PHY mode pins from the RTL8367RB chip, enabling connections to external CPUs for advanced networking features. Refer to Tables 21 and 22 in the chip manual for correct configuration. Inability to access the internet (with local network working) may suggest improper MII expansion pin setup.
2.6 Indicators and Controls  
The board includes multiple LED indicators for power status, link status, and data transmission activity. It also features a reset button and SPI configuration header for development and debugging.
3. Board Structure  
3.1 Dimensions and Layout  
The board measures 100mm × 70.08mm and adopts a 4-layer PCB design to ensure signal integrity and noise immunity. Functional zones are logically separated to optimize routing and thermal paths.
3.2 Enclosure and Installation  
It supports installation with a custom acrylic case. Mounting holes are reserved for easy fixation and modular integration. The case allows pressure mounting of a heatsink on the top side, improving long-term thermal reliability.
4. Usage Instructions  
4.1 Installation Guide  
Install the board into the target system only after soldering and initial testing are completed. When using the MII expansion interface, refer to the datasheet for correct signal mapping and secure soldering to ensure stable communication.
4.2 Debugging Suggestions  
Begin with local network file transfer tests to validate performance and full-port concurrency. If speed degradation occurs, first check for insufficient cooling.
4.3 Safety Precautions  
Ensure power adapter specifications match the required voltage and current before powering on. Avoid prolonged operation in high-temperature environments during debugging to prevent thermal throttling or damage.
Question: Briefly describe the function of this PCB board, covering as many aspects as possible while keeping the language concise.

Answer:
Bladeless Fan Desk Lamp
1. Overview
This PCB is designed for a control system that integrates a bladeless fan and an LED desk lamp, intended for desktop usage scenarios requiring lighting and adjustable airflow. It combines fan and lighting functions with Bluetooth control and display feedback, making it suitable for DIY assembly or embedded smart home modules.
2. Functional Modules  
2.1 Control Core  
The main control unit is based on the STM32F103C8T6 microcontroller, responsible for logic control, user interaction, and Bluetooth communication. It supports domestic alternatives such as the HK32F103C8T6 chip.
2.2 Lighting Driver  
The desk lamp circuit uses a BP2596 chip for constant current LED driving, supporting up to 24W power output with adjustable brightness and color temperature. A BP2525 chip converts 220V AC to 3.3V DC to power the control logic.
2.3 Fan System  
The fan module utilizes a 12V DC brushless motor with a maximum output of 20W. It is powered through a buck converter and regulated by PWM signals. The motor is connected to an ESC (Electronic Speed Controller) to enable stepless fan speed control.
2.4 User Interaction  
The control panel features six physical buttons for adjusting fan speed, brightness, and color temperature. Buttons support both short press (stepped control) and long press (stepless adjustment). The system also supports lighting mode switching via quick power cycling, offering four modes: full brightness, cool white, warm white, and night light.
2.5 Bluetooth Control  
An onboard HC08 Bluetooth module allows remote control via the "HC Bluetooth Assistant" mobile app. Each command maps to specific control functions, enabling wireless adjustment of fan and lighting settings, along with status queries.
2.6 Display Feedback  
An OLED display on the board shows real-time percentage values for brightness, color temperature, and fan speed, enhancing user feedback during operation.
3. Board Structure  
3.1 Circuit Composition  
The system includes three PCBs: a main control board, a button board, and a lamp board. The main control board handles core logic and drivers. The button board connects six user buttons. The lamp board contains 24 LEDs (12 cool white and 12 warm white) arranged evenly.
3.2 3D Design  
The outer shell adopts a rectangular design, consisting of a base, airflow shell, fan blade, outlet, and foldable lamp arm. Designed using Catia, the airflow duct is optimized to multiply airspeed. The top-mounted lamp arm can be folded down for portability and space-saving.
3.3 PCB Design  
All PCBs are designed using Lichuang EDA Professional Edition. The main control board layout positions the fan driver on the left, LED driver on the top right, and control logic with communication modules on the bottom right. Standard component packages are used for ease of soldering and assembly.
4. Instructions for Use  
4.1 Startup Procedure  
After connecting the dual-head power cord, the system is powered on via the panel power switch. Upon startup, the control board enters standby mode with default operation enabled.
4.2 Local Control  
Users can control lighting and fan settings using the six buttons on the panel. Buttons support both stepped and stepless adjustment. Quickly turning off and on the power switch toggles among four lighting modes: full brightness, cool white, warm white, and night light.
4.3 Bluetooth Operation  
To enable Bluetooth control, install the "HC Bluetooth Assistant" app on a smartphone, pair with the device, and configure custom commands. Each command corresponds to a specific function, including brightness, color temperature, fan speed, and status query.
4.4 Safety Notes  
Before powering on, ensure the power adapter voltage and current meet system requirements. Avoid prolonged operation in high-temperature environments. Due to the high power output, proper heat dissipation structures are recommended to ensure long-term stability.
Question: Briefly describe the function of this PCB board, covering as many aspects as possible while keeping the language concise.

Answer:
RTL9210 NVMe 2230 Adapter Board (SSD Enclosure)
1. Overview
This PCB is a compact NVMe 2230 SSD adapter board based on the RTL9210 chip. It supports USB 3.2 GEN2 via a Type-C interface and is suitable for building high-speed solid-state storage modules in space-constrained environments. With a maximum read speed of up to 750MB/s, the board features a single-sided component layout without an enclosure, making it ideal for DIY projects and performance testing.
2. Functional Modules  
2.1 Bridge Controller  
The core component is the RTL9210-VA-CG or RTL9210-VB-CG from Realtek, which bridges USB 3.2 GEN2 to PCIe and supports NVMe protocol. Other variants like RTL9210B-CG and RTL9210BPD-CG have not been tested. If RTL9210B-CG is used, resistor R90 must be soldered (available only on REV4.3).
2.2 Interface Design  
The USB interface is a Type-C female connector, recommended to be pre-soldered via JLC SMT service for better yield. The NVMe interface uses an M-Key 3.2H NGFF connector and only supports 2230-size SSDs. In REV4.3, the spacing between the Type-C and NGFF connectors was increased to avoid mechanical interference.
2.3 PCB Structure  
A board thickness of 1.2mm is recommended to avoid excessive Type-C pin length issues during hot air reflow soldering. The board uses impedance-controlled stackup 7628 and recommends NP-155F as the substrate material. The overall board area is approximately that of two coins, with compact routing and closely grouped interfaces, enabling efficient heatsink attachment.
2.4 Signal Integrity and Limitation  
Under USB 3.2 GEN2 mode, the board achieves up to 750MB/s read speed, but due to compact layout and limited passive cooling, the chip and power regulators heat up quickly, reaching 70°C without a heatsink, potentially causing disconnection. PCIe stability is limited to x1 mode due to signal quality issues on lane1. It is recommended to leave capacitors C41 and C42 unmounted and use the board in PCIe 3.0 x1 mode.
2.5 Soldering Recommendations  
2.5.1 The RTL9210 is a QFN package with no exposed leads, and must be soldered using a hot air rework station  
2.5.2 The NGFF connector is slightly longer than the footprint; pre-tin pads and use hot air for soldering—drag soldering is not recommended  
2.5.3 Apply ample flux, and clean the board thoroughly with PCB cleaner after soldering  
2.5.4 Use solder paste with hot air for best results. If using JLCSMT, it is highly recommended to pay the extra fee to have the Type-C connector pre-soldered, as it is more difficult to hand solder than the NGFF connector
2.6 Stability Recommendations  
Heatsinks on both sides are recommended to manage thermal load and prevent performance throttling.  
SPI Flash must be soldered and firmware updated for stable controller operation.  
Use ≥10V capacitors wherever possible; the voltage divider resistor R2 for 3.3V output is ideally 96kΩ, but 95.3kΩ or 100kΩ are acceptable alternatives.
3. Usage Instructions  
3.1 Compatibility  
This board only supports 2230-size NVMe SSDs. SATA-based SSDs are not supported. Do not insert longer SSDs to avoid damaging the connector. Only RTL9210-VA/VB variants are validated to work stably in REV4 series.
3.2 Firmware and Testing  
Before first use, update the RTL9210 firmware and verify performance using tools such as CrystalDiskMark. If disconnections occur, check the thermal performance and impedance configuration.
3.3 Safety Precautions  
Ensure correct polarity and orientation of all components before soldering. As the board operates bare (without a case), proper ESD protection and handling precautions are necessary.

Answer:
si4844 Mechanical Tuning Full-Band Radio with Digital Display
1. Overview
This PCB is a full-band radio receiver based on the SI4844 RF chip and STM32F103C8T6 microcontroller. It supports mechanical tuning and digital display, with reception capabilities for shortwave (SW), medium wave (MW), and FM bands. With just a 37cm antenna, its sensitivity is comparable to the TECSUN PL600. It is suitable for radio enthusiasts, electronics DIY learners, and developers working on hybrid analog-digital systems.
2. Functional Module Composition  
2.1 Core Components  
The main controller is STM32F103C8T6, supporting development on the Arduino for STM32 platform. The radio reception is handled by the SI4844B20 chip, supporting SW/MW/FM bands, with a mechanical tuner enabling precise frequency control.
2.2 Display Module  
A ST7565 LCD module (purchased from Anxin supplier) is used. Since it lacks native backlight, 4020 RGB LEDs are added to provide colorful backlighting, which also serves as feedback when setting the time.
2.3 Time and Temperature Display  
Date, time, and ambient temperature are displayed using the DS3231 high-precision RTC chip and the DS18B20 temperature sensor.
2.4 Bluetooth and TF Card Playback  
A JL (Jerry) solution-based Bluetooth and TF card playback module is integrated. However, since its buttons are wired in parallel with the radio control buttons, conflicts may arise. This functionality is optional and can be disabled as needed.
2.5 Interference and Layout Optimization  
Due to limited PCB space, the AM antenna is placed close to the MCU, leading to significant interference. Future versions plan to resolve this by repositioning components or adding shielding.
3. Board Structure  
3.1 PCB Layout  
The system consists of a main board and a daughterboard. The layout is based on the official SI4844 design manual and is further customized with added modules for clock, power, display, and playback.
3.2 Interfaces and Expansion  
The board includes the following interfaces and expansion ports:  
3.2.1 Dual TF card slots  
3.2.2 Audio amplifier and speaker connection  
3.2.3 RTC battery holder  
3.2.4 Backlight control circuitry  
3.2.5 Bluetooth audio interface  
3.2.6 I2C bus interface (connected to DS3231 and LCD)  
3.2.7 SPI interface for external device communication  
4. Usage Instructions  
4.1 Arduino Platform Setup  
The program is developed under the Arduino for STM32 platform. The Maple Bootloader must be flashed first to enable compatibility with the Arduino IDE. Recommended libraries include:  
4.1.1 PU2CLR SI4844  
4.1.2 U8G2  
4.1.3 DS3231  
4.2 Programming and Debugging  
An example Arduino program is provided in the attachment and can be used directly. The code is relatively unstructured, so modification is recommended only for users with development experience. Features include FM/MW switching, frequency tuning, LCD display, time and temperature viewing, and backlight control.
4.3 Safety and Debugging Recommendations  
4.3.1 Keep the AM antenna as far away from the MCU and high-frequency noise sources as possible  
4.3.2 Ensure soldering quality to avoid signal instability due to cold joints  
4.3.3 Correct installation of the RTC battery is required to retain time settings when powered off

Bluetooth 5.4 Desktop High-Performance Amplifier with 4.4mm Balanced Headphone Output
1. Overview
This PCB is a compact desktop all-in-one audio board that integrates Bluetooth reception, digital power amplification, and balanced headphone output. Built around the cost-effective QCC3084 module, it is suitable for desktop high-fidelity speaker systems and audiophile-grade headphone amplifier setups. The design emphasizes compact size, high performance, and multi-function integration.
2. Functional Modules  
2.1 Bluetooth Reception and Audio Processing  
The board features the QCC3084 Bluetooth audio SoC, supporting Bluetooth 5.4. It enables high-resolution audio decoding with standards such as APTX-HD and LDAC. Audio input can be received via Bluetooth or USB.
2.2 Digital Amplifier Output  
Equipped with the TAS5760 digital amplifier chip, the system supports up to 100W output power under a 24V power supply. It supports APTX-HD playback with typical distortion as low as 0.0084%. The amplifier channel automatically switches to standby mode when the headphone output is active, preventing conflict.
2.3 Balanced Headphone Output  
A high-performance 4.4mm balanced headphone output is integrated on the board. It supports LDAC playback up to 24bit/96kHz and achieves distortion as low as 0.00065%, capable of driving high-impedance audiophile headphones.
2.4 Control and Button Module  
Front panel controls include:  
2.4.1 Channel switch button (requires device reboot after switching)  
2.4.2 Previous track / Volume down  
2.4.3 Play / Pause / Bluetooth pairing  
2.4.4 Next track / Volume up  
2.5 Power Supply Recommendation  
A DC 24V power supply with 3A to 5A current rating is recommended to ensure stable operation under high-output conditions.
3. Board Structure  
3.1 PCB Layout  
All major components are arranged on a single main board. The layout is compact and optimized for thermal dissipation and signal isolation, effectively reducing interference and crosstalk.
3.2 Interfaces and Expansion  
The board includes the following key interfaces:  
3.2.1 DC 24V power input  
3.2.2 Speaker output terminals  
3.2.3 4.4mm balanced headphone output  
3.2.4 USB input port  
3.2.5 Multiple user control buttons and status indicator circuits

Answer:
EDC_mini Full-Band Radio Receiver (Based on KT0915_DSP Scheme)
1. Overview
This PCB is a compact full-band radio receiver based on the KT0915 DSP radio chip and ATmega328P microcontroller. It supports AM, SW, and FM bands, offering high sensitivity and low power consumption. It is suitable for electronics enthusiasts, portable device developers, and EDC (Everyday Carry) radio designs.
2. Functional Modules  
2.1 Control and Signal Processing  
The main controller is an ATmega328P, which handles control logic and parameter configuration. It utilizes internal EEPROM to preserve settings after power-off. Signal reception is handled by the KT0915 digital DSP chip, which requires only a crystal oscillator and a few capacitors, offering high sensitivity and low power consumption (approx. 20mA operating current).
2.2 Display Module  
The display uses a 1.3-inch SSD1306 OLED screen that presents two lines of information: the upper line shows the current mode and frequency, while the lower line displays RSSI (signal strength), bandwidth, and volume.
2.3 User Interface and Controls  
The front panel features four independent buttons for band switching, audio gain adjustment, and bandwidth control. A rotary encoder on the right side allows tuning, and linear volume control is provided through side buttons.
2.4 Power and Management  
Power management is based on the classic TP4056 charging circuit with a Type-C input and automatic switching between USB and battery power. An AO3401 MOSFET and a Schottky diode manage safe power switching. Two independent LDO regulators help reduce MCU interference with the receiver.
2.5 Audio Output  
The audio amplifier section uses an AD4150 mono AB-class amplifier, supporting both speaker and headphone output. The board includes an auto-switching mechanism: when headphones are inserted, the speaker is muted, and when removed, it resumes speaker playback.
3. Board Structure  
3.1 Dimensions and Form Factor  
The complete device measures 7×4.5×1.5 cm, with a compact layout ideal for EDC pendant-style applications.
3.2 Interfaces  
The board includes the following key interfaces:  
3.2.1 Type-C charging port  
3.2.2 OLED display connector  
3.2.3 Speaker and headphone output  
3.2.4 Rotary encoder and button inputs  
3.2.5 External antenna input and GND lead-out

Answer:
1.5V Wireless Keyboard and Mouse Rechargeable Battery Solution
1. Overview
This PCB is designed to replace standard AA or AAA batteries in wireless keyboards and mice with a rechargeable power solution. The board mimics the shape of traditional batteries and supports direct charging via a Type-C port when connected to a 3.7V lithium battery. It allows easy insertion without requiring structural modifications to the original device.
2 Functional Modules  
2.1 Voltage Regulation Module  
The board uses an ME6206 voltage regulator (SOT23 package, 15XG specification) to replace the commonly used AMS1117. It provides a stable 1.5V output with significantly reduced standby power consumption. The maximum output current reaches 0.5A, which is sufficient for most low-power wireless devices.
2.2 Charging Management Module  
Charging is handled by the TP4056 (SOP8 package), which supports 5V 1A input. The interface is a 6-pin Type-C female port, compatible with general-purpose chargers.
2.3 Cut-to-Fit Design  
The PCB includes marked cut lines for different battery types. Users can cut the board along the guide lines to fit either AA or AAA battery compartments. After cutting, the exposed 5V+ copper on the back should be scraped off (approximately 1mm) to prevent short circuits during insertion.
3 Board Structure  
3.1 Components  
- ME6206 voltage regulator chip  
- TP4056 charging controller  
- 6-pin Type-C female port  
- 1206 capacitors: two 22uF and two 1uF  
- 1206 resistors: one 1.2K and one 1K  
3.2 Soldering-Friendly Layout  
All components use 1206 packages with widened pad spacing for easier manual soldering. The Type-C VBUS and GND pins are rerouted to prevent polarity reversal after cutting and flipping during assembly.
4 Instructions for Use  
4.1 Assembly Steps  
Choose AA or AAA battery mode and cut the PCB accordingly. After cutting, solder the back copper connection pads to the corresponding cut piece to form a complete battery-shaped module.
4.2 Battery Options and Extensions  
Compatible with small lithium batteries or external battery compartments. Can be extended with a wireless charging module to enable drop-and-charge functionality for wireless mice or keyboards.
4.3 Precautions  
After cutting, carefully scrape off the exposed copper on the 5V+ trace at the back to avoid short circuits. The outer metal case of the Type-C connector serves as the 1.5V negative terminal. Although standby current is not precisely measured, the board performs reliably in actual low-power use cases.
5 Real-World Example  
A 280mAh recycled lithium battery was used to power a wireless mouse, demonstrating the effectiveness and practicality of this rechargeable battery solution.

Answer:
XR1075-C1892 Tone Control Board
1. Overview
This PCB is a tone control board based on the BBE XR1075 and C1892 chips. It is suitable for use in DIY audio systems and as a pre-stage tone adjustment module. The board provides functions including volume, treble, bass, and balance adjustment, and supports BBE audio enhancement.
2. Functional Module Composition  
2.1 Power Module  
This board uses a bridge rectifier (ABS210), supporting both AC and DC input, offering strong compatibility.  
The recommended input voltage is 15V to 25V, depending on the capacitor and regulator voltage ratings. 
There are two onboard linear voltage regulators:  
- 7812 provides +12V main power supply for the XR1075 and C1892 dual operational amplifiers  
- 7805 supplies +5V auxiliary power for the C1892  
Large 1mF electrolytic capacitors are used for power filtering to ensure a clean power supply to the audio circuits.
2.2 Signal Path  
The audio signal passes through the input interface, enters the XR1075 via a 1kΩ current-limiting resistor and 3.3μF coupling capacitor.  
The XR1075 handles audio enhancement and clarity improvement, after which the signal is coupled again into the C1892 for three-band tone control (bass, treble, volume) and balance adjustment.
2.3 XR1075 Configuration  
- Fixed working parameters, no dynamic control  
- 154 capacitor (150nF) for low-pass filtering  
- 332 capacitor (3.3nF) for high-pass filtering  
- 100kΩ resistors on pins 4 and 16 for clarity control  
- 100kΩ resistors on pins 5 and 15 for bass compensation  
These components can be fine-tuned according to personal listening preferences.
2.4 C1892 Tone Control Module  
The design retains the core control functions: balance, treble, bass, and volume.  
Other auxiliary circuits are removed for simplification, and the main schematic follows the official PDF reference.  
If balance control is not needed, the left and right audio channels can be bridged using two 10kΩ resistors.
3. Board Structure  
3.1 Control Interface and Layout  
Tone control is realized through four potentiometers, corresponding to:  
- Balance adjustment  
- Treble adjustment  
- Bass adjustment  
- Master volume control  
The center spacing of the potentiometers is 25mm, 25mm, and 35mm respectively, suitable for front panel layout.
3.2 Input and Output Interfaces  
- Input supports stereo (L/R) signal with GND  
- Output is also stereo (L/R) with GND
3.3 PCB Dimensions and Layout  
Board dimensions are approximately 120mm × 80mm (based on potentiometer spacing).  
The layout is compact and logical, with a clear separation between power and signal areas to minimize interference.
4. Usage Instructions  
4.1 Installation Recommendations  
- Input power is recommended to be DC 18V or AC 15–18V via an appropriate power adapter  
- All capacitors should be high-quality low-ESR electrolytic or film capacitors  
- Operational amplifiers are socketed and replaceable. XR1075 is a dedicated BBE chip, and NEC C1892CT is a classic three-band tone control chip
4.2 Tuning Suggestions  
- To enhance bass strength, consider lowering the 100kΩ resistor on pins 5/15  
- To improve vocal clarity, consider increasing the 100kΩ resistor on pins 4/16  
- Potentiometers with a resistance of 20kΩ are recommended for smooth linear tuning response

Desktop HiFi Amplifier
1. Overview
This PCB is a compact desktop Bluetooth amplifier based on the TPA3255 and AB5301A. It is suitable for desktop audio systems, offering independent bass and treble control, automatic Bluetooth input switching, and front audio output. Each channel supports up to 300W output power with excellent sound quality and extremely low distortion.
2. Functional Modules  
2.1 Audio Signal Processing Module  
This module handles signal source input and switching (Bluetooth or RCA) and performs pre-stage audio processing. The volume knob includes a button function that allows users to disconnect the current Bluetooth connection via double-click. Both bass and treble can be adjusted independently.
2.2 Bluetooth Module  
The Bluetooth section is relatively simple, mainly responsible for receiving Bluetooth audio signals and controlling the input channel switching. When Bluetooth audio is active, the system automatically prioritizes it as the input source.
2.3 Power Amplification Module  
Audio amplification is handled by a TPA3255DDVR chip. This high-performance amplifier delivers up to 2×300W stereo output with ultra-low THD+N (Total Harmonic Distortion + Noise). The circuit design follows the official TI application notes.
2.4 Power Supply Module  
The power section includes comprehensive protections and voltage regulation:  
- Overcurrent protection and reverse polarity protection for system safety  
- A LM5164DDAR buck converter generates a stable 12V supply for NE5532 and TPA3255  
- A separate LDO generates 3.3V to power the Bluetooth chip  
3. Input and Output Interfaces  
Input Interfaces:  
- Bluetooth audio input  
- RCA analog audio input  
Output Interfaces:  
- 3.5mm front audio output  
- Speaker power output terminals  
4. Control and Operation  
- Independent treble and bass adjustment knobs  
- Volume knob supports push-button functionality with double-click to disconnect Bluetooth  
- Automatic input source detection and switching  
5. Application Notes  
This board is ideal for desktop HiFi systems, bookshelf speakers, and DIY multimedia setups. It combines high performance with a compact form factor, making it suitable for users seeking premium audio quality in limited space.

Answer:
SL2.1A-Based USB HUB Expansion Board Documentation
1. Overview
This PCB is a USB HUB expansion board based on the SL2.1A chip, supporting 1 USB-A input and 4 USB-A outputs. It is suitable for scenarios requiring USB expansion such as desktop computers, Raspberry Pi, etc. The board features power filtering and overcurrent protection, making it ideal for cost-sensitive but practical applications.
2. Functional Modules
2.1 USB Input Module  
The board uses a standard USB-A male connector for both power and data input.  
A self-recovery fuse (6V 2A) is connected in series at the input to provide overcurrent protection and enhance safety during operation.
2.2 Power Filtering Module  
The board integrates multiple electrolytic capacitors (e.g., 220μF, 104, etc.) to filter and stabilize both input and output voltages, reducing power noise.  
An LED indicator circuit composed of R1 and LED1 is provided to show the power status and aid debugging.
2.3 USB HUB Controller  
The main controller is the SL2.1A chip, which manages USB signal distribution and address assignment, supporting up to four USB downstream devices.  
Key pin connections include:  
- Pins 1D~4D connect to the USB output ports  
- DM/DP handle the main USB data input signals  
- Internal oscillator can be enabled by connecting Pin 16 to GND (cost-saving option)  
- Optional external 12MHz crystal oscillator (X1) is supported for improved accuracy
2.4 USB Output Ports  
Four USB-A female ports are provided for output.  
Each output is accompanied by decoupling capacitors to ensure stable power delivery and improve electrical characteristics.
3. Board Structure
3.1 PCB Dimensions and Layout  
Board dimensions are approximately 66.675mm × 48.260mm.  
A double-sided layout maximizes space efficiency. The controller is placed centrally, while USB ports are aligned along the edges for convenient access and mounting.
3.2 Connectors and Silkscreen  
The silkscreen clearly labels functional modules and pin directions for ease of assembly and maintenance. The top side includes version information: “Design by XQ Ver 1.0-2”.
4. Usage Instructions
4.1 Power Supply  
The board can be powered directly via a computer USB port or an external USB power source. A supply of 5V/2A or higher is recommended.  
The LED indicator lights up when power is supplied correctly.
4.2 Oscillator Configuration  
For applications with low-speed requirements, the internal oscillator can be used by grounding Pin 16.  
For better communication stability, a 12MHz external oscillator (X1) can be installed.
4.3 Notes  
- Ensure correct USB port orientation to avoid short circuits  
- Observe capacitor polarity; solid capacitors are recommended for durability  
- Consider adding mounting holes along the PCB edges for secure enclosure integration

Answer:
HomeAssistant Ultra-Low-Power WiFi Temperature and Humidity Sensor
1. Overview
This PCB is a sensor acquisition board designed based on the Ai-M62-CBS module from Ai-Thinker. It is intended for indoor environmental monitoring tasks within HomeAssistant. The board supports WiFi6 connection and MQTT protocol for direct integration with HomeAssistant. It performs periodic temperature and humidity acquisition and reporting, ideal for long-battery-life scenarios.
2. Functional Module Composition
2.1 Main Control Module  
Powered by the Ai-M62-CBS module, which features a WiFi6-capable SoC with MQTT support and deep sleep capability. The main controller performs periodic tasks: wake-up, sensor acquisition, data reporting, and sleep. The default interval is 1 hour.  
It exposes rich GPIOs, I2C, ADC, and other interfaces for expansion.
2.2 Power and Charging Module  
Powered by a 14500 lithium battery (3.7V 1000mAh) with the following features:  
- Onboard lithium charging IC with USB 5V input  
- Charging status indicators (charging/full)  
- Battery protection IC for overcharge/overdischarge protection  
- Power path management to auto-switch between USB and battery  
- Low-dropout regulator (LDO) to provide a stable 3.3V supply
2.3 Sensor Module  
Uses the SHT30 digital temperature and humidity sensor, communicating via I2C. A MOSFET (Q2) controls power to the sensor to reduce standby consumption.  
Includes recommended external components such as pull-up resistors, decoupling capacitors, and reset circuitry.
2.4 Battery Voltage Measurement Module  
Composed of Q5 and Q6, this module enables controllable voltage division and sampling for measuring battery voltage. After voltage division, the signal is fed into the main controller's ADC. Q6 allows enabling/disabling the sampling path to prevent static leakage.
2.5 Communication Interfaces  
- Onboard USB port: for firmware flashing and charging  
- I2C header: standard 4-pin for additional I2C sensors  
- Rd-03L radar interface: compatible with Ai-Thinker radar modules  
- Reserved pads for single-wire communication and 1.1V DCDC expansion
2.6 Debug and Flashing Module  
- Onboard RST and BOOT buttons for firmware flashing and reset  
- Onboard USB-to-TTL circuit supporting CH340 for serial flashing  
- Status LEDs for sensor power, system activity, etc., to aid debugging
3. Board Structure
3.1 Interface and Layout  
- Clear separation of power management and sensor control sections  
- All interfaces are accessible via pin headers or solder pads for easy development  
- Compact and modular layout with power and logic zones separated  
- Clear silkscreen labeling for quick reference and maintenance
3.2 Input/Output Interfaces  
- USB Type-C or Micro-USB: supports 5V input and firmware updates  
- BAT+/BAT- terminals for 14500 lithium battery  
- Exposed signals: I2C, ADC, SENSOR_EN, VOL_CHECK, 3.3V, GND, etc.  
- All connectors clearly labeled for ease of use
3.3 PCB Dimensions and Layout  
- Approximate size: 80mm × 50mm  
- Modular zones for control, power, sensing, and measurement  
- Marked with "HomeAssistant Sensor Acquisition Board" and labeled test points  
- Uses black solder mask with red signal traces and full ground fill
4. Instructions for Use
4.1 Power-Up  
Can be powered directly by a lithium battery or temporarily by USB. A 5V/2A power supply is recommended.
4.2 Acquisition Logic  
The board automatically runs a periodic routine: wake, acquire, report, sleep. The default interval is 1 hour. Average current consumption is around 16μA.  
Firmware handles all logic; no manual interaction is required during normal operation.
4.3 Firmware Flashing  
Use the onboard Boot and Reset buttons to enter flash mode. Firmware can be updated via USB-TTL (CH340). The board will auto-run after flashing.
4.4 Debugging Tips  
Monitor data reception via MQTT tools or HomeAssistant logs. For hardware tuning (sampling intervals, resistor values), refer to the schematic.

Answer:
Mini Semiconductor Cooling Device
1. Overview
This PCB serves as the control core for a mini semiconductor-based cooling device, utilizing a TEC1-12706 Peltier cooler and a CPU heatsink. It is suitable for compact, portable cooling applications.
2. Functional Modules
2.1 TEC Power Control Module  
The TEC1-12706 is powered via a MOSFET (TX40N06B), controlled by the MCU through an optocoupler for electrical isolation.  
- Input Voltage: 12V  
- Max Current: 4.7A
2.2 Fan Power Control Module  
A separate MOSFET (WST3400A) controls the CPU fan, ensuring effective heat dissipation on the hot side of the Peltier device. Full-speed operation is recommended.
2.3 MCU Core Module  
The board uses an HK32F030MF4P6 microcontroller for core logic, including I/O control, ADC sampling, and I2C communication.
2.4 Current/Voltage Sensing Module  
- Current sensing is achieved using an INA180A2IDBVR amplifier and a 5mΩ shunt resistor (two 10mΩ resistors in parallel on the actual PCB)  
- Voltage is measured using a resistor divider network for input/output voltage monitoring
2.5 Display and Interaction Module  
- Temperature sensing via DS18B20, measuring the cold side of the Peltier  
- TM1650 driver controls 7-segment LED displays via I2C, showing temperature, voltage, and current  
- Three onboard push buttons for user interaction  
- External I/O headers:  
  - 1x4P (I2C) for display connection  
  - 1x5P for LED indicators
2.6 Power Management Module  
- DC-DC Converter: ME3116AM6G (input 12V → output 5V) for system supply  
- LDO Regulator: AMS1117-3.3 to provide 3.3V for MCU and peripherals
3. Board Structure
3.1 PCB Size and Layout  
Approximate dimensions: 80mm × 80mm  
The layout is modular and organized, with dedicated layers for signal and power to enhance EMC and stability.
3.2 Silkscreen and Mounting  
- Silkscreen annotations identify all functional modules and ports  
- Four M3 mounting holes are placed at the corners for secure installation and heatsink attachment
4. Instructions
4.1 Power Supply  
A high-power 12V power adapter (rated >60W) is recommended to ensure stable operation of the TEC module.
4.2 Notes  
- The cooling performance depends heavily on heat dissipation at the hot side; always ensure active cooling  
- Optional air ducting may enhance airflow but offers limited cooling boost in practice  
- Mist cooling using an atomizer and ducting is not effective due to condensation forming droplets inside the tubes

Answer:
Mini USB Bluetooth Digital Amplifier
1. Overview
This PCB is a compact USB Bluetooth digital amplifier. It utilizes the XMOS XU208 digital interface and QCC5125 Bluetooth module to receive USB and Bluetooth audio signals. The amplifier chip is Infineon's MA2304DNS digital Class-D amplifier, capable of delivering up to 2x37W output power with high-fidelity audio, suitable for desktop HiFi audio systems.
2. Functional Modules  
2.1 Audio Input  
USB Input: Supports USB 2.0 high-speed audio input with sampling rates of 32kHz, 44.1kHz, 48kHz, 88.2kHz, 96kHz, and 192kHz, and bit depths of 16bit, 24bit, and 32bit.  
Bluetooth Input: Supports LDAC, AptX, and other Bluetooth audio formats, with up to 96kHz sampling rate and 24bit bit depth.
2.2 Power Amplification  
Class-D Amplifier: Low power requirements, high efficiency, and low heat generation. Efficiency exceeds 50% at 1W output.  
Output Power: Supports 2x37W stereo output (THD = 10%), compatible with a minimum 4Ω load, suitable for desktop speakers.  
THD+N: 0.05% at 5W, 1kHz.
2.3 Enclosure and Panel Design  
Dimensions: Approximately 10.5cm × 10cm × 3cm.  
Power Input: DC-005 interface supporting 12V to 18V input.  
Binding Posts: 4mm crystal terminals for easy wire insertion/removal.  
Audio Input: USB Type-C interface, replacing traditional bulky Type-B for modern compatibility.  
Bluetooth Antenna: SMA RF interface with detachable antenna support.
2.4 User Interaction Design  
Volume Control: Rotary encoder for digital volume adjustment, avoiding abrupt limits.  
Bluetooth Controls: Buttons for Bluetooth playback and volume adjustment, allowing easy track switching without a phone.  
Display: Intended to show sampling rate, system status, and volume (not implemented).
3. System Architecture  
Audio Reception: XMOS XU208 and QCC5125 modules handle USB and Bluetooth audio input respectively.  
Audio Decoding: Dual I2S signals are selected via SN74LVC244 and fed into the MA2304DNS for decoding and amplification.  
Clock Synchronization: Output clock from XU208 is realigned using a D flip-flop to eliminate jitter.  
Bluetooth Signal: QCC5125 uses an IPEX connector to enhance antenna signal quality.
4. Key Components  
Amplifier Chip: MA2304DNS, a high-efficiency digital Class-D amplifier ideal for desktop HiFi.  
Digital Interfaces: XMOS XU208 for USB, QCC5125 for Bluetooth.  
Main Controller: STM32F051, responsible for decoder control, volume adjustment, and audio source switching.  
Power Management: DC-DC converter ME3116AM6G (12V to 5V) and LDO regulator AMS1117-3.3 supply voltage to the system.
5. Conclusion  
This mini USB Bluetooth digital amplifier features modern USB Type-C and Bluetooth audio input, driven by the MA2304DNS for exceptional sound quality and high output power. Compact and thoughtfully designed, it is ideal for desktop HiFi applications and a great addition to any home audio setup.

Answer:
IP5389 Mobile Power Supply Board
1. Overview
This PCB is designed based on the IP5389 chip for mobile power supply applications. It supports 2/3/4/5 series-connected lithium cells and provides a maximum output power of 100W with extensive fast-charging protocol compatibility.
2  Functional Module Composition
2.1  Multi-Port Input/Output Module  
Supports multiple USB ports:  
- 2 x USB-A output ports  
- 1 x USB-C input/output port  
- 1 x USB-B or Lightning input port or alternate USB-C
2.2  Fast Charging Protocol Support Module  
The onboard IP5389 integrates the following protocols:  
- QC2.0 / QC3.0 / QC3+ output fast charge  
- FCP / AFC / SCP / VOOC input/output fast charge  
- DRP try.SRC mode  
- PD2.0 / PD3.0 bidirectional input/output, PPS supported (20mV steps)

- Battery charging current up to 5A  
- Adaptive current adjustment  
- Configurable cell voltages: 4.1V, 4.15V, 4.2V, 4.3V, 4.35V, 4.4V

- ≥97% efficiency at 5V 2A synchronous discharge

- Compatible with 88, 188 digital tubes

2.5  Auxiliary Function Module  
- Automatic phone plug/unplug detection  
- Fast charge status indicator  
- Battery temperature monitoring via NTC  
- Light load standby mode supported
2.6  Safety Protection Module  
- Input over-voltage / under-voltage protection  
- Output over-current / over-voltage / short-circuit protection  
- Battery overcharge / over-discharge / over-current protection  
- IC over-temperature protection  
- Charge/discharge temperature protection via NTC  
- 4kV ESD protection, 30V withstand voltage on input pins including CC/DP/DM
2.7  BOM Optimization Module  
- Integrated power MOS driver  
- Single inductor supports both charging and discharging
3  Board Structure
3.1  PCB Dimensions and Layout  
Board size: approx. 98.5mm × 42.8mm  
Compact dual-sided routing design. The core chip is centered, with ports distributed along top and bottom edges for heat dissipation and wiring convenience.
3.2  Silkscreen and Identification  
Silkscreen labels clearly indicate battery terminals, USB ports, buttons, and battery indicators for easier assembly and debugging.
4  Usage Instructions
4.1  Power-Up  
The board can be powered via USB or an external DC supply. A recommended power supply of no less than 5V/2A is advised.

Answer:
mSATA Solid-State Mass Production Adapter Board
1. Overview
This PCB is designed for mSATA solid-state drive (SSD) card unlocking and mass production testing. It is based on the INIC-1618L USB-to-SATA bridge controller, converting a USB interface into an mSATA interface for data transfer and device programming.
2 Functional Module Composition
2.1 Main Controller Interface and Bridging Scheme  
This design uses the INIC-1618L controller, implementing USB 2.0 protocol to SATA conversion for mSATA SSD access.  
Power is regulated from 5V (USB input) to 3.3V using the RY3430 power management chip to supply the mSATA SSD.
2.2 Power Control and Indicator Module  
The board includes a master power switch for convenient on/off control during mass production scenarios.  
Three onboard indicator LEDs are provided:  
Main controller read/write status  
5V supply status for the controller board  
3.3V SSD power supply status
2.3 Interface Module  
USB input uses a USB Type-C connector, supporting USB 2.0 data rate.  
The mSATA slot provides a standard interface for SSD connection.  
Additional solder pads are reserved for debugging or control extensions.
2.4 Assembly and Component Recommendations  
PCB thickness is recommended to be 1mm.  
mSATA slot height: recommended 4mm or 4.2mm models.  
Mounting nuts: M2 threaded knurled standoffs, 3mm length, dual-threaded type.  
Screws: M2 × 3mm or M2 × 4mm, standard cross-head flat screws.  
Power switch: recommended model MSS-22D18, DIP type, dual-row six-pin. Search “22D18” and ensure DIP package is selected.
3 Board Structure Description
3.1 PCB Size and Layout  
The board layout is compact:  
Left area: USB and power input  
Center: main controller and power conversion module  
Right area: mSATA slot  
High-frequency signal traces are short and direct, and power lines are appropriately wide, ensuring good EMC performance.
3.2 Silkscreen and Labels  
Clear silkscreen labels for POWER input, voltage specs, and controller info.  
Signal and power traces are labeled with “LOW SPEED” and “SATA SSD” for debugging assistance.  
Key interfaces are marked with text for convenient wiring and debugging.
4 Usage Notes and Remarks
4.1 USB Type-C Note  
As this design uses USB 2.0, the Type-C port does not pull down the CC lines (no 5.1kΩ resistors). Normally, this does not affect usage.  
If strict Type-C compliance is required, users can manually add two 5.1kΩ pull-down resistors.
4.2 BOM Updates  
Specific part models and purchasing suggestions will be posted in pinned comments or included in a BOM version of the PCB file with annotated component information.

Answer:
IP5356 Fast-Charging Camping Light Conversion Board (2-Cell Battery)
1. Overview
This PCB is designed to convert a transparent dual 21700 battery power bank into a camping light. It uses the IP5356-188-BZ as the main controller chip. The chip supports fast-charging protocols (excluding VOOC), enabling up to 22.5W input/output through the TYPE-C port and up to 22.5W output through the TYPE-A port. It also integrates a 188 digital display tube for battery level indication.
2. Functional Modules
2.1 Power Input/Output  
The TYPE-C port supports 22.5W PD/QC input and output  
The TYPE-A port supports 22.5W fast-charging output  
The LED light board draws power from the A2 port, providing only basic voltage output without fast-charging support.
2.2 Display and Indicators  
A 188 digital tube is integrated for displaying battery levels.  
Short press the button to check the battery or enable output; long press to toggle the LED light board.  
Accurate RCAP resistor settings are required to ensure proper battery level indication. Typical resistor values:  
22kΩ: 10000mAh  
33kΩ: 15000mAh  
44kΩ: 20000mAh  
2.3 PCB Structure and Dimensions  
The PCB is designed as a panel and needs to be manually separated. A board thickness of 1.0mm is recommended. The main board and LED board are connected using 7mm-height copper standoffs.  
To minimize cost, it is recommended to reuse components included in the original kit. Alternatively, components can be sourced separately.
3. Usage Instructions  
Long press: Turn the LED light board on or off  
Short press: Light up the digital tube to show remaining battery or enable output  
Powering the LED light board does not affect battery statistics, but fast-charging will be disabled during use  
Only use the IP5356-188-BZ chip; do not substitute with IP5356-LED-BZ series
4. Additional Recommendations  
Search for a compatible transparent dual-cell (21700) power bank case

Answer:
RA7F2E1-based Network Fingerprint Lock
1. Overview
This PCB is designed to create a fingerprint network lock using the ESP-12F (ESP8266) as the core communication module, along with BLINKER for mobile unlocking and fingerprint registration. The board also includes a WS2812 module for displaying indoor temperature, humidity, and various lighting effects, in addition to the DHT22 sensor, eliminating the need for an LCD display in traditional password locks and enhancing security.
2. Functional Modules
2.1 ESP-12F and BLINKER  
ESP-12F (ESP8266) is a commonly used WIFI communication module. It communicates with the MCU via HTTP protocol through BLINKER, which is easy to operate and supports mobile voice assistants. By configuring buttons, features like fingerprint registration, door unlocking, and 12 customizable lighting effects can be activated.
2.2 Voice Assistant Module  
To support offline voice functionality, a SU-03T voice assistant module has been added. This module supports custom firmware, providing more customizable functions such as serial commands, PWM, and GPIO control.
2.3 SPI Driver and WS2812  
The SPI driver controls the WS2812 using the duty cycle to transmit data, such as sending a WS2812 signal with a 60% duty cycle, which meets the high-level signal requirements of WS2812.
2.4 Stepper Motor Module  
An 4988 stepper motor module is integrated to provide support for unlocking scenarios requiring high torque. Unlike servo motors, stepper motors can handle larger loads and provide precise control, making them ideal for high-demand locking applications.
2.5 Serial Download and Communication  
A CH9102X serial chip is used for high-speed downloading with a maximum speed of 4M, and a 2X3 header is provided for downloading to either ESP8266 or RA2E1, as well as for serial communication and debugging.
2.6 Power Management  
A DCDC step-down power supply is used to power the MCU, WS2812B, and servo. Additionally, LDOs provide power to the fingerprint module and the ESP8266/voice assistant. This ensures ample power supply and includes a USB protection circuit, supporting both USB and 12V power connections simultaneously.
2.7 Fingerprint Module  
The Haier FPM383C fingerprint module is used, supporting automatic fingerprint verification. The recognition logic is triggered when a finger is placed on the module, and the recognition result is returned via the serial interface.
3. Usage Instructions  
Long press: Turn the LED light board on or off  
Short press: Light up the digital tube to show remaining battery or enable output  
Powering the LED light board does not affect battery statistics, but fast-charging will be disabled during use  
Only use the IP5356-188-BZ chip; do not substitute with IP5356-LED-BZ series

Yuzuki LOHCC HDMI Loop-Out Capture Card
1. Overview
This PCB is a low-cost HDMI loop-out capture card based on the MS2109 + MS9332 chipset. It supports high-definition video input and loop-out, features a Type-C interface, and costs as little as 34 RMB. It is suitable for entry-level scenarios involving video signal distribution and capture.

2.1 HDMI Input and Loop-Out  
Equipped with HDMI IN and HDMI OUT ports, this board supports a 1-in-2-out configuration, simultaneously sending input signals to both a display and a capture module. Key features include:
- Support for HDMI 4Kx2K@30Hz and HDMI 2.0
- Support for YCbCr420 4Kx2K@60Hz loop-out

3. Usage Instructions  
HDMI IN: Connect to video source devices such as PCs or gaming consoles  
HDMI OUT: Connect to display devices like monitors or TVs  
USB: Connect to a capture terminal (e.g., PC or Raspberry Pi) for video capture and recording  
Driver-free setup; automatically recognized as a UVC device, compatible with most operating systems and video software

Answer:
96-Key Keyboard Based on ATMEGA32U4
1. Overview
This PCB design is based on the ATMEGA32U4 microcontroller and uses a 96-key layout, supporting open-source firmwares such as QMK. Each side has one C-type port, making it ideal for DIY keyboard projects. This design is for personal DIY use only and not for commercial purposes.
2. Functional Modules
2.1 ATMEGA32U4 Microcontroller  
The ATMEGA32U4 microcontroller is the core of this keyboard, supporting both VQFN-44 and QFP-44 packages. It communicates with a computer via USB and supports custom keyboard input functions. The board is compatible with open-source firmwares like QMK and VIA, allowing for easy customization of features.
2.2 Keyboard Layout  
The keyboard features a 96-key layout, including letters, numbers, function keys, and special function keys. Note that the PCB design does not include mounting holes, allowing users to modify the case design as needed.
2.3 RGB Lighting Effects  
The board features WS2812 reverse-mounted RGB matrix key lights, which support multiple lighting effects. Since the lights require significant current, it is recommended to use an 800-1000mA fuse (Component F13) to prevent damage.
2.4 USB and C Ports  
There are two C-type ports, one on each side of the board, for easy connectivity and data transfer. These ports make the keyboard compatible with a wide range of devices.
2.5 Firmware and Programming Support  
The firmware supports QMK, VIA, and VIAL, allowing users to customize keymaps and macros. VIA configuration files (VIA.json) are provided, along with the firmware source code (kb.zip).
3. Usage Instructions  
- Firmware: Upload the kb_default.hex to enable the custom firmware. VIA configuration and programming are also supported.
- Hardware: Connect the keyboard to a computer or device via the C port for USB communication.
- Lighting: The WS2812 RGB matrix lights provide various lighting effects, controllable through the firmware.

Answer:
Mini 2-in-1 Power and Headphone Amplifier V2.0
1. Overview
This PCB is a mini 2-in-1 amplifier, updated to version 2.0 with a primary focus on improving audio quality. It is built around the HK32F030M microcontroller and integrates the SGM3157 analog switch, CAT5171 digital potentiometer, LTC722 operational amplifier, TPA6138 headphone amplifier, and TPA3116 class-D power amplifier.
2. Functional Modules
2.1 Power Supply  
Supports PD or DC input, with a voltage range of 12V–24V.
2.2 Amplifier and Headphone Output  
- Integrates both a class-D power amplifier and a headphone amplifier  
- Two audio input channels selectable; supports switching between headphone and speaker output  
- Digital volume control
2.3 Audio Quality Enhancements  
- Removed PT7313 (present in the previous version) due to its negative impact on headphone audio quality  
- Replaced with SGM3157 analog switch and CAT5171 digital potentiometer to control input selection and volume, allowing independent adjustment of left and right channels  
- Adopted LTC722 op-amp for single-ended to differential amplification before feeding TPA3116, improving noise performance  
- Changed TPA6138 mute control to PNP logic to eliminate occasional POP noise during power-on/off  
- Upgraded 3.3V LDO to higher-performance MIC5205
2.4 Display and Protection Features  
- Includes I2C interface for connecting common OLED screens (e.g., SSD1306)  
- Integrated over-voltage, under-voltage, and over-temperature protection for safe operation
3. Hardware Fix Note  
- The TPA6138 headphone amplifier originally lacked coupling capacitors. A 2.2µF capacitor was added in series with R56 and R61, followed by a 51KΩ pull-down resistor to AGND (fixed on 2021.10.26). No wire patching required.
4. Software  
Firmware version: AMP-2.0-2021.10.23  
All hardware features are verified and functional. Basic features are displayed on the OLED main screen. Volume and balance control logic are still under development. OLED supports inverse display and progress bar. Protection features (OVP, UVP, OTP) are fully implemented.
